zh.lifeSummary: 當William Clark Jr.於1656年7月3日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特時，他的父親Lieutenant William Clarke47歲，母親Sarah Strong11653歲。他於1680年6月15日與Hannah Strong在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1723年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農被列為judge of the probate court, district of windham。他去世於1725年5月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文勒班農享年68歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農笛卡爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Clark Jr.于1656年7月3日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特时，他的父亲Lieutenant William Clarke47岁，母亲Sarah Strong11653岁。他于1680年6月15日与Hannah Strong在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1723年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農被列为judge of the probate court, district of windham。他去世于1725年5月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文勒班農享年68岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農笛卡爾 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Clark Jr. was born on 3 July 1656, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Lieutenant William Clarke, was 47 and his mother, Sarah Strong, was 11653. He married Hannah Strong on 15 June 1680, in Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. In 1723, his occupation is listed as judge of the probate court, district of windham in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 9 May 1725, in Lebanon, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 68, and was buried in Old Cemetery, Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: William Clark Jr.が1656年7月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターで生まれた時，父Lieutenant William Clarkeは47歳，母Sarah Strongは11653歳であった。1680年6月15日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアノーサンプトンでHannah Strongと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1723年，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンでの職業はjudge of the probate court, district of windhamと記録されている。彼は1725年5月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンレバノンで68歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンオルド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Clark Jr.(이)가 1656년 7월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lieutenant William Clarke(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Strong(은)는 11653세였다. 그는 1680년 6월 15일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 노르섬프턴에서 Hannah Strong(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1723년, 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌에서 judge of the probate court, district of windham이었다. 그는 1725년 5월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 레바넌에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌 드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Clark Jr. nasceu em 3 de julho de 1656, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Lieutenant William Clarke, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Strong, tinha 11653 anos. Ele casou-se com Hannah Strong em 15 de junho de 1680, em Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1723, sua profissão aparece como judge of the probate court, district of windham em Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 9 de maio de 1725, em Lebanon, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Old Cemetery, Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Clark Jr. родился 3 июля 1656 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Lieutenant William Clarke было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Strong, 11653. Он женился на женщине по имени Hannah Strong 15 июня 1680 г. в местности нортамптон, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. В 1723 году он работал по профессии judge of the probate court, district of windham в местности лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 9 мая 1725 г. в местности лебанон, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности олд кеметири, лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.
